extraction.one_line_summary: BAGIOLI Andrea timed his move perfectly with 3.5km to go after INEOS grinded the breakaway down, neutralizing late attacks from BUITRAGO and CAIRO on the Montjuïc circuits.
tactical_patterns: INEOS grinding pace neutralized 12-man breakaway to create CARAPAZ time-gaining scenario, exposing their GC calculation prioritized time gaps over stage win.
ALMEIDA attacked to defend podium from QUINTANA with HIGUITA following, revealing UAE prioritized team defense over individual stage glory.
BAGIOLI exploited hesitation in 6-rider sextet when JUMBO brought back 12-15 chasers, proving rolling terrain sprint superiority for Quick-Step.
GROVES targeted intermediate sprint at start rather than final circuits, intelligent points jersey calculation avoiding GC battleground.

rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: locked onto CARAPAZ wheel to neutralize every move, defending 16-second lead with tactical discipline under sustained INEOS grinding pressure
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: attacked from leader's jersey but struggled slightly on final Montjuïc ramps, revealing form limitations after earlier stage win
rider_signals.BAGIOLI Andrea: positive: perfectly timed attack on rolling terrain when sextet hesitated after chase group junction, demonstrates elite positioning instincts
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: late individual effort showed exceptional climbing form, suggesting Bahrain has additional GC weapon beyond team role
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: attacked specifically to defend podium from QUINTANA rather than win, revealing UAE's defensive tactical prioritization
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: appeared dropped then recovered but couldn't attack for podium, suggesting climbing legs not sharp enough for decisive moves
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: strategic intermediate sprint target to secure points jersey avoiding GC finale risks shows mature tactical calculation
